pass1_value.note: The official Authentication reference page (meta/description) states Attio uses API keys and OAuth 2.0 for authentication; SDK examples (npm/pypi) also show api_key usage.

final_value.note: Attio's official Authentication docs state that interacting with the Attio API requires an Attio API key (sent as `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>`), and also document OAuth 2.0 for integrations across multiple workspaces (access tokens also sent via the Bearer Authorization header).

pass1_value.note: The API reference overview page (meta/description) mentions 'endpoints, models, REST examples' and there are reference pages for product objects (e.g. Collections, People) showing multiple endpoints.

final_value.note: The official API Overview states the Attio API is a RESTful API using standard HTTP methods over HTTPS with JSON responses, with a v2 base URL (https://api.attio.com/v2) and resources including Lists, Objects, Records, Notes, Tasks, and more—indicating a broad REST surface.
